perturbation.original_text: All information disclosed by one party to the other to evaluate the Project UW Oshkosh Revised 12/16/19 and/or Purpose that is designated in writing as “Confidential” at the time of disclosure or if disclosed orally is designated in writing as “Confidential” within fifteen (15) days of disclosure is “Confidential Information.” Confidential Information does not include information which: (a) was known by the Receiving Party prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party; (b) is generally available in the public domain or thereafter becomes available to the public through no act of the Receiving Party; (c) is independently discovered by an employee, agent, or representative of the Receiving Party who had no knowledge of the Confidential Information disclosed; or 2. is made availab le to the Receiving Party as a matter of lawful right by a third party .
perturbation.changed_text: All information disclosed by one party to the other to evaluate the Project UW Oshkosh Revised 12/16/19 and/or Purpose that is designated in writing as “Confidential” at the time of disclosure is “Confidential Information.” Confidential Information does not include information which: (a) was known by the Receiving Party prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party; (b) is generally available in the public domain or thereafter becomes available to the public through no act of the Receiving Party; (c) is independently discovered by an employee, agent, or representative of the Receiving Party who had no knowledge of the Confidential Information disclosed; or 2. is made availab le to the Receiving Party as a matter of lawful right by a third party .
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'or if disclosed orally is designated in writing as “Confidential” within fifteen (15) days of disclosure', the agreement now contradicts itself. Previously oral disclosures could become confidential with written confirmation. Now, only information designated 'Confidential' in writing at the time of disclosure is considered Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the intent of the initial definition and other implicit references where oral disclosures might be considered later confidential with supporting documentation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All information disclosed by one party to the other to evaluate the Project UW Oshkosh Revised 12/16/19 and/or Purpose that is designated in writing as “Confidential” at the time of disclosure is “Confidential Information.” Confidential Information does not include information which: (a) was known by the Receiving Party prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party; (b) is generally available in the public domain or thereafter becomes available to the public through no act of the Receiving Party; (c) is independently discovered by an employee, agent, or representative of the Receiving Party who had no knowledge of the Confidential Information disclosed; or 2. is made availab le to the Receiving Party as a matter of lawful right by a third party .
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for oral disclosures to become confidential if designated in writing within 15 days. The changed text removes this possibility, stating that only information designated as 'Confidential' in writing at the time of disclosure is considered confidential. This creates a direct conflict because the contradicted text (which is identical to the changed text in this instance) now only defines confidential information as such if it was designated in writing at the time of the original disclosure, effectively nullifying any possibility for oral disclosures to later become confidential. This is a strong contradiction as it alters the definition of 'Confidential Information' and breaks compatibility with the original intent.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees to disclose Confidential Information only to their respective employees, agents, or representatives who have been determined to have a need to know and have been advised of their obligation to comply with the terms of this Agreement. To the extent allowed by the law applicable to the Receiving Party, including, but not limited to, for UW -Oshkosh, Wis. Stat. §§ 893.82 & 895.46,” the Receiving Party will be liable for any breach of this agreement by any of its employees, agents, affiliates or representatives that receive access to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees to disclose Confidential Information only to their respective employees, agents, or representatives who have been determined to have a need to know. To the extent allowed by the law applicable to the Receiving Party, including, but not limited to, for UW -Oshkosh, Wis. Stat. §§ 893.82 & 895.46,” the Receiving Party will be liable for any breach of this agreement by any of its employees, agents, affiliates or representatives that receive access to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and have been advised of their obligation to comply with the terms of this Agreement', the text no longer explicitly states that employees, agents, or representatives need to be informed of the NDA terms. This creates an internal contradiction since the clause discussing liability for breaches assumes individuals with access to the Confidential Information have been made aware of their obligations under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees to disclose Confidential Information only to their respective employees, agents, or representatives who have been determined to have a need to know. To the extent allowed by the law applicable to the Receiving Party, including, but not limited to, for UW -Oshkosh, Wis. Stat. §§ 893.82 & 895.46,” the Receiving Party will be liable for any breach of this agreement by any of its employees, agents, affiliates or representatives that receive access to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the requirement that employees, agents, or representatives be advised of their obligation to comply with the terms of the agreement. This creates a contradiction because the original text, and thus the contradicted text, implies that these individuals should be aware of the agreement's terms before the Receiving Party can be held liable for their breaches. The change makes it unclear if the Receiving Party is liable for breaches committed by individuals who were not informed of their obligations, directly contradicting the intent of the liability clause.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party will return or destroy Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party upon termination of the Agreement. The Receiving Party’s designated representative may maintain one copy of all Confidential Information for the purpose of addressing any claim that may be brought under this Agreement and to comply wit h any other legal or recordkeeping requirements, and neither party will be obligated to destroy any Confidential Information that is stored electronically on back -up systems or computer hard drives after a file is deleted, but any such electronic informati on will continue to be subject to the terms of confidentiality under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party will return or destroy Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party upon termination of the Agreement. The Receiving Party’s designated representative may maintain one copy of all Confidential Information for the purpose of addressing any claim that may be brought under this Agreement, and neither party will be obligated to destroy any Confidential Information that is stored electronically on back -up systems or computer hard drives after a file is deleted, but any such electronic informati on will continue to be subject to the terms of confidentiality under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of 'and to comply with any other legal or recordkeeping requirements' creates a contradiction. While the paragraph states the designated representative may maintain a copy to address claims, it no longer explicitly accounts for retention to comply with legal or recordkeeping needs, potentially conflicting with legal requirements for document retention. Other legal obligations might mandate keeping the documents when the initial statement suggest that the representative can hold it for only addressing claims.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party will return or destroy Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party upon termination of the Agreement. The Receiving Party’s designated representative may maintain one copy of all Confidential Information for the purpose of addressing any claim that may be brought under this Agreement, and neither party will be obligated to destroy any Confidential Information that is stored electronically on back -up systems or computer hard drives after a file is deleted, but any such electronic informati on will continue to be subject to the terms of confidentiality under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows the Receiving Party's representative to keep a copy of confidential information for addressing claims and complying with legal/recordkeeping requirements. The changed text removes the allowance for legal/recordkeeping requirements. The contradicted text allows that the Receiving Party’s representative may maintain one copy of all Confidential Information for the purpose of addressing any claim that may be brought under this Agreement, and neither party will be obligated to destroy any Confidential Information that is stored electronically on back -up systems or computer hard drives after a file is deleted, but any such electronic information will continue to be subject to the terms of confidentiality under this Agreement. Removing the allowance causes an in-text contradiction because it implicitly suggests that retention is *only* permissible for claims, creating ambiguity and potential conflict with other legal obligations for document retention, making enforcement unclear and introducing ambiguity.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information will be used only to evaluate the Project and/or Purpose and will be provided in writing and marked "Confidential."
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information will be used only to evaluate the Project and/or Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and will be provided in writing and marked "Confidential."', the definition of how confidential information is handled is now incomplete. Section 1 still requires information to be designated in writing as Confidential. Thus, the omission creates an in-text contradiction because section 6 suggests that it will be used to evaluate the Project, but without the marking requirement, it is unclear if the information is actually Confidential based on the main definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information will be used only to evaluate the Project and/or Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that Confidential Information 'will be provided in writing and marked "Confidential."'. The changed text removes this requirement. The contradicted text repeats that 'Confidential Information will be used only to evaluate the Project and/or Purpose.' Without the requirement to mark the information as confidential in writing, it is now unclear if the information being used is actually Confidential Information, as per the original definition. This creates a direct conflict because the contradicted text expects information to be Confidential, which is no longer guaranteed under the new language in the Changed Text, thus the Omission creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Disclosing Party represents and agrees (i). it has the right to share its Confidential Information with the Receiving Party, (ii). the Receiving Party is authorized to use Confidential Information it receives from the Disclosing Party for the Purpose or Project, and (iii). to the extent allowed by the law applicable to the Disclosing Party, the Disclosing Party will be liable for any breach by the Disclosing Party of the representations in subparts and (ii).
perturbation.changed_text: The Disclosing Party represents and agrees (i). it has the right to share its Confidential Information with the Receiving Party, (ii). the Receiving Party is authorized to use Confidential Information it receives from the Disclosing Party for the Purpose or Project.
perturbation.explanation: Removing '(iii). to the extent allowed by the law applicable to the Disclosing Party, the Disclosing Party will be liable for any breach by the Disclosing Party of the representations in subparts and (ii).' removes the clause holding the disclosing party liable for breaches of their representations regarding the right to share and authorize use of Confidential Information. This creates an in-text contradiction. It removes a key enforcement mechanism.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Disclosing Party represents and agrees (i). it has the right to share its Confidential Information with the Receiving Party, (ii). the Receiving Party is authorized to use Confidential Information it receives from the Disclosing Party for the Purpose or Project.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes a clause (iii) that holds the disclosing party liable for breaches of representations regarding the right to share and authorize use of confidential information. The changed text omits this clause, removing the liability aspect. The contradicted text repeats the representations from the original text. Because the Changed Text removes the liability clause, it directly contradicts the intent of the representations made in the contradicted text as the Disclosing party can breach the contract with no ramifications. This is an Omissions - In Text Contradiction as it removes a key enforcement mechanism.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall supersede and prevail over any other prior arrangements, either oral or written, as to the Confidential Information received under this Agreement. U W Oshkosh Revised 12/16/19 T his Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties relative to this subject matter and shall not be amended, except in a writing signed by the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall supersede and prevail over any other prior arrangements, either oral or written, as to the Confidential Information received under this Agreement. U W Oshkosh Revised 12/16/19 T his Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties relative to this subject matter.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and shall not be amended, except in a writing signed by the parties', the agreement no longer stipulates the method for amending the agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction. The agreement previously stated it was the entire agreement, but also stated that it could only be amended in writing. Now, by removing the amendment clause, it implies the agreement can be amended in any way (oral, written, implied), thus creating an ambiguity about the entire agreement and contradicting its initial state.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall supersede and prevail over any other prior arrangements, either oral or written, as to the Confidential Information received under this Agreement. U W Oshkosh Revised 12/16/19 T his Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties relative to this subject matter.
perturbation.justification: The original text contained a clause stating that the agreement 'shall not be amended, except in a writing signed by the parties.' The changed text removes this clause. The removal of this clause creates an ambiguity in the contradicted text. Since the agreement states that it is the entire agreement but the amendment clause is gone, it now implies the agreement can be amended in any way (oral, written, implied), thus creating an ambiguity about the entire agreement and contradicting its initial state that it constitutes the entire agreement, fulfilling the condition for an Omissions - In Text Contradiction.
